# Claude Code prompt — [Fix] Hide duplicate Quote preview block (PR-9 UI cleanup follow-up #2)

*Paste below the line. Generated by Cowork, 2026-05-29.*

---

You are working on `GH-Mantova/ProjectOperations`. Read `CLAUDE.md` and follow it.

## Background — what's still wrong after PR #254

PR #254 removed the duplicate **tab labels** from the secondary tab array, but it left the **whole secondary render block** in place. The Quote panel still renders TWO sections stacked:

1. **Top section (canonical):** "Editing IS-T100-R1 — Acme Infrastructure DRAFT" header with the QUOTE CONTENTS row and the full canonical tab strip (Cost Summary | Scope items | Provisional Sums | Cost Options | Assumptions | Exclusions | Preview | Terms & Conditions). In view mode this is the Quote versions panel + the version row.
2. **Bottom section (orphan from PR #253, still mounted):** a floating "Edit" button, a "Cost summary" card with a "Recalculate" link, and a second tab strip (Cost Summary | Assumptions | Exclusions | Terms & Conditions | Generate Quote) with read-only-ish content underneath.

Owner's words: *"tabs are still split, they have not fully merged into one section like I asked … there are 2x edit buttons now … the second one should not show, neither to the recalculate button that is there … this recalculate should disappear now (your last PR made the changes to the total automatic as I enter the % or $, which is much better solution)"*

So three clear cleanups:

1. **Delete the floating "Edit" button.** Canonical Edit lives on the version row in view mode and Save/Cancel in the editor when editing — the second one is leftover from PR #253.
2. **Delete the "Recalculate" button on the Cost Summary card.** PR #255 made adjustments auto-recompute on input — Recalculate is dead UX.
3. **Hide the secondary preview block entirely during edit mode**, AND consolidate the view-mode tab strip onto the canonical one so there is ONE quote section, not two stacked.

## Operating rules

1. **Single branch:** `fix/hide-duplicate-quote-preview-block`. Stay on it.
2. **5 phases**, each committed and pushed with prefix `[pr15-phase-N]`.
3. **After each phase, run `pnpm build` and `pnpm lint`.** Both must pass.
4. **Hard ABORT:** 3 failed Edits in a row → STOP and report. Don't loop.
5. **Do NOT reason about file watchers.** Whitespace mismatch in `old_string`, every time.
6. **The target file is `ClientQuotesPanel.tsx` (still ~320 lines after PR #254 cleanup).** Smaller now, so Edits should be tractable.

## Phase 0 — Resume detection + branch setup

```
git fetch origin
git log --all --oneline | grep -E "\[pr15-phase-" | head -10
```

- No matches → fresh branch off `main`:
  ```
  git checkout main
  git pull
  git checkout -b fix/hide-duplicate-quote-preview-block
  ```
- Otherwise checkout existing branch and pull. Confirm clean tree.
- Announce starting phase.

## Phase 1 — Inventory the residual orphan block

**Already done if:** `git log --oneline | grep -q "\[pr15-phase-1\]"` returns true.

**Scope:** Read-only mapping phase. Output is a short note committed as a doc.

1. Open `apps/web/src/pages/tendering/ClientQuotesPanel.tsx` and `apps/web/src/pages/tendering/QuoteTab.tsx`.
2. Find the JSX that renders the **second** "Edit" button. It's the one that floats above the secondary tab strip and is NOT inside the Quote versions row. Search for `>Edit<` and identify each occurrence — note line numbers and which component they live in.
3. Find the JSX that renders the **"Recalculate"** link/button on the Cost summary card. Search for `Recalculate`.
4. Find the JSX that renders the **secondary tab strip** (Cost Summary | Assumptions | Exclusions | Terms & Conditions | Generate Quote). Even after PR #254 emptied the duplicate tab array, the wrapper `<nav>` / `<div>` may still be rendering 4–5 tab buttons that the array still contains, OR the wrapper itself wasn't deleted in Phase 7 of PR #254. Identify it.
5. Identify the parent component that hosts this orphan block. It may be a separate component file (e.g. `QuotePreview.tsx`, `QuoteReadOnlyView.tsx`) imported into `QuoteTab.tsx`, or it may be inline JSX inside `QuoteTab.tsx`.
6. Write `docs/pr-prompts/pr-15-orphan-block-inventory.md` with:
   - File + line range of the second "Edit" button.
   - File + line range of the "Recalculate" button.
   - File + line range of the secondary tab strip wrapper + the tab content blocks it conditionally renders.
   - Component name(s) hosting the orphan block.
   - A removal plan: for each of the three artifacts, whether to delete inline JSX, delete an imported component, or wrap in `{!isEditing && …}`.
7. Commit:
   ```
   git add docs/pr-prompts/pr-15-orphan-block-inventory.md
   git commit -m "[pr15-phase-1] docs: inventory residual orphan Quote preview block"
   git push -u origin fix/hide-duplicate-quote-preview-block
   ```

**Stop. Verify the note is on origin before moving on.**

---

## Phase 2 — Delete the floating "Edit" button

**Already done if:** `git log --oneline | grep -q "\[pr15-phase-2\]"` returns true.

1. Per the inventory note, locate the orphan `<button>Edit</button>` (or equivalent) that's NOT on the Quote versions row.
2. Delete it along with its container if the container becomes empty.
3. Delete the now-unused handler/state hooks it referenced if they're not used elsewhere. `pnpm lint` will flag unused vars — fix them inline.
4. Verify the CANONICAL Edit button on the Quote versions row still works (clicking it puts the panel into edit mode).
5. Build + lint. Commit:
   ```
   git add -A
   git commit -m "[pr15-phase-2] fix: delete orphan floating Edit button"
   git push
   ```

---

## Phase 3 — Delete the "Recalculate" button

**Already done if:** `git log --oneline | grep -q "\[pr15-phase-3\]"` returns true.

**Why:** PR #255 made the cost summary auto-recompute on every Adjustment % / $ input change. The manual Recalculate button is dead UX and confusing.

1. Locate the "Recalculate" anchor/button on the Cost summary card.
2. Delete it and its `onClick` handler.
3. If the handler called a `recalculate()` method on the service or a hook, and nothing else calls it, leave the method itself in place (other consumers may exist) — but remove the dead button binding.
4. Build + lint. Commit:
   ```
   git add -A
   git commit -m "[pr15-phase-3] fix: remove dead Recalculate button (auto-recalc landed in PR-255)"
   git push
   ```

---

## Phase 4 — Hide the secondary preview tab strip during edit mode

**Already done if:** `git log --oneline | grep -q "\[pr15-phase-4\]"` returns true.

**Scope:** When the user clicks Edit and the canonical editor panel is showing, the secondary preview block (its remaining tabs + content) must NOT also be rendered. Use a single `{!isEditing && …}` gate around the whole secondary wrapper.

1. Find the wrapper `<div>` / `<section>` containing the secondary tab strip and all its conditionally-rendered content blocks.
2. Wrap the whole thing:
   ```tsx
   {!isEditing && (
     <SecondaryPreviewBlock … />
   )}
   ```
   …where `isEditing` is whatever boolean the canonical editor uses (search the file for the existing `setIsEditing` or `editMode` state hook).
3. **Do NOT** also condition on the active tab — the whole secondary block disappears in edit mode regardless of which sub-tab is selected.
4. Build + lint. Commit:
   ```
   git add -A
   git commit -m "[pr15-phase-4] fix: hide secondary preview block when editing"
   git push
   ```

---

## Phase 5 — Consolidate view-mode tab strip onto the canonical one

**Already done if:** `git log --oneline | grep -q "\[pr15-phase-5\]"` returns true.

**Scope:** Even in view mode, the user should see only ONE tab strip — the canonical one (Cost Summary | Scope items | Provisional Sums | Cost Options | Assumptions | Exclusions | Preview | Terms & Conditions). The secondary strip's remaining tabs (Cost Summary | Assumptions | Exclusions | Terms & Conditions) are subset duplicates and should be removed.

Decision tree per secondary-strip tab:

- **Cost Summary** — canonical Cost Summary tab in view mode should render the same read-only table the secondary block was rendering. If it doesn't yet (i.e. canonical view-mode Cost Summary is currently empty or different), port the secondary block's read-only Cost Summary content into the canonical Cost Summary tab's view-mode render path.
- **Assumptions / Exclusions** — same pattern. Canonical view-mode should show the read-only list. If it already does, just delete the secondary version. If it doesn't, port it.
- **Terms & Conditions** — canonical Terms & Conditions tab (added in PR #252) should render the clause list. In view mode, render clause bodies as plain text; in edit mode, the editable textareas with override-highlight + revert. If the view-mode read-only render is missing, add it.
- **Generate Quote** — PR #254 Phase 6 already moved this to a header button. Confirm it's still wired up and the secondary "Generate Quote" tab is gone.

After this phase, in view mode the user should see:
- Top: Quote versions panel with the version row.
- Below: ONE canonical tab strip.
- No floating Edit button, no Recalculate button, no second tab strip.

1. Per the decision tree, port any missing view-mode read-only renders into the canonical tabs.
2. Delete the secondary tab strip's wrapper and all its remaining tab buttons.
3. Delete any now-unused state (the secondary active-tab `useState`, etc.).
4. `pnpm lint` will flag dead code — clean it up.
5. Build + lint. Commit:
   ```
   git add -A
   git commit -m "[pr15-phase-5] fix: consolidate to one canonical tab strip in view mode"
   git push
   ```

## Final step — Open the PR

**Already done if:** a PR exists on GitHub for branch `fix/hide-duplicate-quote-preview-block`.

- `gh pr create` with:
  - Title: `[Fix] Hide duplicate Quote preview block — one canonical tab strip per mode`
  - Body: reference PR #253 (introduced the duplicate), PR #254 (removed tab labels but not the container), PR #255 (made Recalculate dead). List the 5 phases with one-line summaries.
  - Reviewer: `GH-Mantova`.
  - **Do NOT auto-merge.** Owner verifies live.

## Live-verification checklist for owner

On IS-T100-R1 — Acme Infrastructure:

- [ ] View mode: ONE tab strip visible (Cost Summary | Scope items | Provisional Sums | Cost Options | Assumptions | Exclusions | Preview | Terms & Conditions). No floating Edit button below. No Recalculate button on Cost summary card.
- [ ] View mode: canonical Cost Summary tab shows the read-only cost summary table (same content the orphan strip used to show).
- [ ] View mode: canonical Terms & Conditions tab shows the read-only clause list.
- [ ] Click Edit on the version row → editor panel appears with its canonical tab strip. No secondary preview block visible below.
- [ ] Adjust % or $ → cost lines + total update live (no Recalculate needed).
- [ ] Generate Quote button still works from the header.

## Scope — do NOT do

- Do NOT touch the cost-allocation math (PR #255 is authoritative).
- Do NOT touch the T&C clause editor (PR #252 + PR #255 are authoritative).
- Do NOT touch the Scope items DnD/grouping (PR #253 territory).
- Do NOT introduce new sub-tabs.
- Do NOT make a single Edit call that rewrites >100 lines.

## If you get stuck

Stop and report:
- Which phase you're on.
- `git diff HEAD --stat`.
- The error or whitespace mismatch.
- What you've tried.

Whitespace mismatch in `old_string`, every time. Re-Read fresh, copy exact surrounding context, retry once, report.
